demos[0].description: Bachelor's thesis on detecting polyps in colonoscopy images with deep learning, using CycleGAN and SPADE to generate synthetic training data when real labels were scarce.
demos[0].lead: Train Faster R-CNN, RetinaNet, and SSD detectors on colonoscopy data, augmented with synthetic images from CycleGAN and SPADE generative models. Hyperparameter search via Optuna & Ray Tune.
demos[0].metaDescription: Bachelor's thesis on polyp detection in colonoscopy with deep learning, using CycleGAN and SPADE for synthetic data. Interactive PyTorch + Optuna demo.
demos[0].title: Bachelor's Thesis — Polyp Detection
demos[1].aboutDescription: Draculin was developed for the BitsXLaMarató 2023 hackathon, where it reached the finals. The app uses a Google LLM-powered chatbot for health guidance and computer vision to analyze images for menstrual health tracking. The frontend is built with Flutter (supporting iOS, Android, and web), while the backend runs on Django with image inference capabilities.
demos[1].description: Health-tracking app with an AI chatbot and computer vision assistance, built in Flutter and Django as a BitsXLaMarató 2023 finalist.
demos[1].lead: Walk through Draculin: a Flutter + Django health-tracking app with an AI chatbot and computer vision assistance. BitsXLaMarató 2023 finalist.
demos[1].metaDescription: Flutter + Django health-tracking app with an AI chatbot and computer vision. BitsXLaMarató 2023 finalist — browse the UI and interactive flows.
demos[10].description: Automated planning course project: a PDDL domain for booking trips with flights, hotels, numeric constraints, and metric minimization.
demos[10].lead: Browse a PDDL travel-agency domain — flights, hotels, stay duration, and a numeric interest metric to minimize. Inspect the Extension 2 files, download .pddl sources, or run the planner when an ENHSP backend is available.
demos[10].metaDescription: Automated planning course project: a PDDL travel-agency domain with flights, hotels, numeric constraints, and metric minimization. Interactive planner demo.
demos[10].title: Travel agency (PDDL)
demos[11].description: AI course project framing disaster relief as local search, with helicopters rescuing groups from rescue centers via hill climbing and simulated annealing.
demos[11].lead: Solve disaster-relief assignment as local search — helicopters rescuing groups from centers on an interactive 2D map. Compare hill climbing vs simulated annealing with 3 heuristics and 6 successor functions.
demos[11].metaDescription: AI course project framing disaster relief as local search: helicopters rescuing groups via hill climbing and simulated annealing. Solid.js + FastAPI interactive solver.
demos[12].description: Machine learning course practica: training and comparing nine sklearn model families to predict hypothyroidism from clinical data.
demos[12].lead: Explore an end-to-end hypothyroidism classification pipeline with 9 sklearn model families. The interactive demos below run real trained models directly in your browser.
demos[12].metaDescription: Machine learning course practica comparing 9 sklearn model families to predict hypothyroidism. Interactive notebook, live predictor, and k-NN PCA classifier.
demos[12].title: Machine Learning — Hypothyroid
demos[13].aboutDescription: A university project for the "Projectes de Programació" course at FIB-UPC. The core domain logic is written in pure Java and features complex data structures and algorithms for filtering and recommending items based on past user ratings and item attributes (K-Nearest Neighbors and Collaborative Filtering concepts). The web interface is powered by Spring Boot and server-side rendered with Thymeleaf and Bootstrap 5.
demos[13].description: Software engineering course project: a content-based recommendation system built in Java with Spring Boot and Thymeleaf, including a full web interface.
demos[13].lead: Browse a content-based recommendation system built in Java with Spring Boot and Thymeleaf — modelling users, items, and ratings to deliver personalized picks.
demos[13].metaDescription: Software engineering course project: a Java Spring Boot content-based recommender with a Thymeleaf web UI. Browse the flow live or as a static mockup.
demos[13].title: Recommendation System
demos[14].aboutDescription: Originally lab exercises for the CAIM (Search and Analysis of Information) course at FIB-UPC, covering PageRank, Zipf/Heap laws, TF-IDF, Rocchio, MapReduce, and graph analysis. This full-stack web app reimplements the PageRank and Zipf algorithms in Python (FastAPI) with a Vanilla TypeScript + D3.js frontend — no UI framework, just plain TypeScript managing the DOM directly.
demos[14].description: Information retrieval course labs: computing PageRank over the global airport network and verifying Zipf's Law on text corpora.
demos[14].lead: Explore core information retrieval ideas interactively: run PageRank on the global airport network with a world-map visualization, and fit Zipf's Law on real text corpora.
demos[14].metaDescription: Information retrieval course labs: PageRank on the global airport network and Zipf's Law on text corpora. Interactive D3 world map and curve-fitting tool.
demos[14].title: CAIM IR Explorer
demos[15].badge: Knowledge-Based Systems · HTMX + Alpine.js + FastAPI · FIB-UPC
demos[15].description: Knowledge-based systems course project: an expert system trip planner originally written with CLIPS production rules, reimplemented in Python.
demos[15].lead: Answer 10 questions on trip type, budget, duration, and hotel preferences, and get a full itinerary with cities, hotels, activities, transport, and cost. A CLIPS expert system reimplemented in Python with an HTMX + Alpine.js frontend.
demos[15].metaDescription: Knowledge-based systems course project: a CLIPS-style expert system trip planner reimplemented in Python with an HTMX + Alpine.js frontend.
demos[15].title: Trip Planner (Expert System)
demos[16].badge: Parallel Computing · Preact + Canvas + WASM · FIB-UPC
demos[16].description: Parallel computing course labs in C with OpenMP: Mandelbrot, heat equation, 4-way merge sort, π estimation, and speedup benchmarks.
demos[16].lead: Run five OpenMP labs compiled to WebAssembly: the Mandelbrot set, the heat equation with Jacobi iteration, 4-way merge-sort, numerical π, and parallel speedup benchmarks.
demos[16].metaDescription: Parallel computing course labs in C with OpenMP — Mandelbrot, heat equation, 4-way merge sort, π, and speedup — compiled to WebAssembly in-browser.
demos[16].title: Parallel Computing Labs
demos[17].description: Robotics course labs covering odometry, wall-following, 3D arm forward kinematics (Puma560 / 3-Link), trajectory planning, sensors, and EKF state estimation.
demos[17].lead: Step through six interactive robotics visualizations built with Ember.js and Babylon.js: mobile-robot odometry with laser scans, wall-following, 3D arm kinematics (Puma560/3-Link), trajectory planning, sensor dashboards, and EKF state estimation.
demos[17].metaDescription: Robotics course labs: odometry, wall-following, 3D arm kinematics, trajectory planning, sensors, and EKF. Interactive Ember.js + Babylon.js dashboard.
demos[17].title: Robotics Dashboard
demos[18].description: Algorithms and data structures coursework covering 18 classical algorithms across graphs, sorting, search, and combinatorial problems.
demos[18].lead: Step through 18 classic algorithms — Dijkstra, Prim's MST, BFS/DFS, sorting, N-Queens, 2-opt TSP, and more — with interactive visualizations built in Qwik and Canvas.
demos[18].metaDescription: Algorithms and data structures coursework — 18 classical algorithms across graphs, sorting, search, and combinatorics. Interactive Qwik + Canvas visualizer.
demos[18].title: Algorithm Visualizer
demos[19].description: Computer graphics course labs originally written against desktop OpenGL, covering vertex transforms, Phong lighting, procedural textures, fragment patterns, and geometry shader emulation.
demos[19].lead: Edit WebGL2 shaders live — vertex transforms, Phong lighting, procedural textures, fragment patterns, geometry effects, and more. Ports the FIB-UPC Gràfics course labs to the browser.
demos[19].metaDescription: Computer graphics course labs originally for desktop OpenGL, covering vertex transforms, Phong lighting, procedural textures, and geometry effects. Live WebGL2 shader playground.
demos[19].title: Computer Graphics Shader Playground
demos[2].description: BitsXLaMarató 2022 winner: a tool to segment the aorta in ultrasound videos with Mask R-CNN and reconstruct it in 3D to help detect aneurysms.
demos[2].lead: 🏆 Winning project of the BitsXLaMarató 2022 hackathon. Run the aorta pipeline live: abdominal ultrasound → Mask R-CNN segmentation → 3D mesh reconstruction → aortic diameter estimation.
demos[2].metaDescription: BitsXLaMarató 2022 winner: Mask R-CNN aorta segmentation and 3D reconstruction for aneurysm detection. Interactive PyTorch + OpenCV inference demo.
demos[3].aboutDescription: Originally a MATLAB project for the Computer Vision course at FIB-UPC. This browser demo is a port of the original pipeline to JavaScript — it uses adaptive binarization, morphological filtering, connected component analysis, and template matching to detect license plates and read their characters, all running client-side with no external dependencies.
demos[3].description: Computer vision project to locate and read license plates from car photos, originally built as a classical MATLAB pipeline.
demos[3].lead: Detect and read license plates from car photos. Pick a sample or upload your own and watch the 3-stage pipeline — plate detection, character segmentation, OCR — run entirely in your browser.
demos[3].metaDescription: Computer vision project to locate and read license plates, originally in MATLAB and ported to TypeScript. Runs entirely in the browser.
demos[3].title: License Plate Detection
demos[4].aboutDescription: Written in C++11, the game engine runs 4 AI players simultaneously on a 2D grid. The custom AI (AIJohnnyMelavo) uses BFS pathfinding to prioritize weapon collection, enemy hunting, and resource gathering. Each player controls warriors (fighters) and builders (constructors) who compete for money, food, guns, and bazookas across day/night cycles.
demos[4].description: Data structures course project: a grid-based game where C++ bots compete as warriors and builders in a class-wide tournament.
demos[4].lead: Watch C++ AI bots compete in "The Purge" — a grid-based strategy game from the Data Structures and Algorithms course at FIB-UPC.
demos[4].metaDescription: Data structures course project: C++ AI bots competing on a grid. Interactive Canvas replay viewer with bundled or live matches.
demos[4].title: Programming Game Viewer
demos[5].aboutDescription: Originally built with ANTLR4 and Python for a compilers course at FIB-UPC. The language supports procedures, recursion, arithmetic, lists, conditionals, loops, and musical note playback. This browser version uses a hand-written parser and the Web Audio API for sound synthesis.
demos[5].description: Compilers course project: JSBach, a small programming language where you describe musical pieces as code, built with an ANTLR4 grammar.
demos[5].lead: Write music with code: JSBach is a small language with procedures, variables, loops, and conditionals that plays back musical notes. Try the examples or write your own.
demos[5].metaDescription: Compilers course project: JSBach, a small music programming language built with ANTLR4. Write code and play it live in a Web Audio interpreter.
demos[5].title: JSBach Interpreter
demos[6].aboutDescription: A complete e-commerce platform with product catalog, category browsing, shopping cart, checkout flow, and an admin order management panel. Features include dynamic product pages, cart persistence, and order processing.
demos[6].description: Web applications course project: a full e-commerce store built in PHP and MySQL, covering product catalog, shopping cart, and checkout flow.
demos[6].lead: Browse an e-commerce store built in PHP and MySQL: explore products by category, add to cart, and walk through the checkout flow.
demos[6].metaDescription: Web applications course project: full PHP + MySQL e-commerce store with catalog, cart, and checkout. Browse against the live backend or a static rebuild.
demos[6].title: Tenda Online
demos[7].aboutDescription: Originally a C++17 project for the PRO2 course at FIB-UPC. The algorithm computes pairwise distances between species using k-mer frequency analysis, then iteratively merges the closest pair using Weighted Pair Group Method with Arithmetic Mean (WPGMA) to build a phylogenetic tree.
demos[7].description: Programming course project: implementing WPGMA clustering in C++ to build phylogenetic trees from species gene sequences.
demos[7].lead: Build phylogenetic trees step by step. Add species with gene sequences, inspect the k-mer distance matrix, and watch WPGMA merge the closest clusters into a dendrogram.
demos[7].metaDescription: Programming course project: WPGMA clustering in C++ building phylogenetic trees from gene sequences. Interactive D3 dendrogram.
demos[7].title: WPGMA Clustering
demos[8].aboutDescription: Originally a C++ project for the Algorithmics course at FIB-UPC, solving the MPIDS problem with greedy heuristics, simulated annealing, and CPLEX ILP. This full-stack web app reimplements the algorithms in Python (FastAPI backend) with an Elm + D3.js frontend providing an interactive force-directed graph visualization via ports.
demos[8].description: Algorithms project on the Minimum Positive Influence Dominating Set problem, solved with greedy heuristics and simulated-annealing-style local search.
demos[8].lead: Find the Minimum Positive Influence Dominating Set on a graph — the smallest set where every vertex has at least half its neighbors inside. Pick a sample or load your own, then run greedy or local search.
demos[8].metaDescription: Algorithms project on the Minimum Positive Influence Dominating Set, solved with greedy and local-search heuristics. Interactive D3 force-directed graph editor.
demos[8].title: MPIDS Solver
demos[9].aboutDescription: Originally a Python project for the Algorithmics course at FIB-UPC, studying phase transitions in random graphs using NetworkX. This full-stack web app wraps the original Python/NetworkX logic in a FastAPI backend with a Lit (Web Components) + Canvas + D3.js frontend for interactive graph visualization and Monte Carlo phase transition charts.
demos[9].description: Algorithms research project studying connectivity and complexity phase transitions on binomial, geometric, and grid random graphs.
demos[9].lead: Watch random graphs transition from connected to disconnected as you remove nodes or edges. Generate binomial (Erdős–Rényi), geometric, or grid graphs, apply percolation, and see the phase transition in real time.
demos[9].metaDescription: Algorithms research on connectivity and complexity phase transitions in binomial, geometric, and grid random graphs. Interactive percolation animations and Monte Carlo charts.
demos[9].title: Graph Phase Transitions
experience[0].bullets.0: Delivered Deloitte's first Physical AI project: real-time manufacturing anomaly detection on live motor production lines and a warehouse digital twin with camera-based package tracking, built on NVIDIA Omniverse, Isaac Sim, and the Metropolis stack (DeepStream, TAO, Triton)
experience[0].bullets.1: Designed and deployed AI and data infrastructure across AWS, Azure, and GCP — edge inference, streaming pipelines, and cloud-hosted training and model serving
experience[0].bullets.2: Embedded in the Generalitat de Catalunya's Data Governance Office (OGD) to help define the institution-wide data strategy — established data catalogues, mapped asset lineage with Informatica, and delivered governance advisory to cross-departmental teams across the public administration
experience[1].bullets.0: Built pricing prediction models as part of a 4-person AI and data team supporting insurance product analytics
experience[1].bullets.1: Automated SQL Server workflows with PowerShell to streamline recurring data processing and reporting tasks
experience[1].bullets.2: Developed geospatial analysis projects in R / RStudio, using map data to support location-based business insights
ui.about.label: About
ui.about.p1: Hi! I'm Pol — a Computer Science Engineer from Barcelona. I graduated from UPC's Facultat d'Informàtica de Barcelona (FIB) and now work as an AI & Data Consultant at Deloitte, where most of my days are spent at the intersection of AI, data platforms, and the messy reality of making them useful.
ui.about.p2: What I actually enjoy is building stuff — exploring new tools, figuring out how they fit together, and helping my team ship things we're proud of. I care more about solving the problem than about picking the trendiest stack, though I'm happy when both line up.
ui.about.p3: Outside of work you'll usually find me climbing, deep in a videogame, or pretending my Vim keybindings make me faster than I really am. Here's some of the tech I've been using lately:
ui.about.title: A bit about me

work_projects[0].description: The world's first Physical AI project delivered at Deloitte. Built on NVIDIA Omniverse, Isaac Sim, and the Metropolis stack (DeepStream, TAO, Triton) to detect manufacturing anomalies on live motor production lines in real time, plus a warehouse digital twin with camera-based package tracking.
work_projects[0].title: Physical AI Anomalies Detection
work_projects[1].description: End-to-end software that parses PDF resumes, standardizes them into structured JSON, and indexes them in a RAG database. Allows semantic querying to find the best candidates for a job description and vice versa.
work_projects[1].title: E2E AI Talent Matching Platform
